description: Increased risk of bleeding when combined. Monitor INR closely and watch for signs of bleeding.
recommendation: Use alternative antiplatelet agent or adjust warfarin dose. Monitor INR frequently.

description: Risk of lactic acidosis and acute kidney injury. Metformin should be held before and after contrast procedures.
recommendation: Hold metformin 48 hours before and after contrast administration. Monitor renal function.

description: Risk of hyperkalemia (elevated potassium levels). Can cause cardiac arrhythmias.
recommendation: Monitor potassium levels regularly. Avoid potassium supplements unless specifically indicated.

description: Increased risk of myopathy and rhabdomyolysis. Gemfibrozil inhibits statin metabolism.
recommendation: Use alternative fibrate (fenofibrate) or reduce statin dose. Monitor CK levels.

description: Verapamil increases digoxin levels, risk of digoxin toxicity and arrhythmias.
recommendation: Monitor digoxin levels. May need to reduce digoxin dose. Use alternative calcium channel blocker if possible.

description: NSAIDs reduce lithium clearance, increasing risk of lithium toxicity.
recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs. Use acetaminophen for pain. Monitor lithium levels if NSAIDs are necessary.

description: Increased bleeding risk. NSAIDs inhibit platelet function and can displace warfarin from protein binding.
recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs. Use acetaminophen instead. If NSAIDs necessary, use lowest dose for shortest duration.

description: NSAIDs reduce methotrexate clearance, increasing toxicity risk.
recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs. Use acetaminophen for pain. Monitor renal function and methotrexate levels.

description: Omeprazole inhibits clopidogrel activation, reducing its antiplatelet effect.
recommendation: Use alternative PPI (pantoprazole) or H2 blocker. Monitor for stent thrombosis.

description: Clarithromycin inhibits simvastatin metabolism, increasing myopathy risk.
recommendation: Use alternative antibiotic or statin. Monitor CK levels if combination necessary.

description: Ciprofloxacin increases theophylline levels, risk of toxicity.
recommendation: Monitor theophylline levels. May need dose reduction. Use alternative antibiotic if possible.

description: Complex interaction - phenytoin increases warfarin metabolism initially, then may increase warfarin levels.
recommendation: Monitor INR closely. May need warfarin dose adjustment. Consider alternative anticonvulsant.

description: Fluconazole increases warfarin levels, increasing bleeding risk.
recommendation: Monitor INR closely. May need warfarin dose reduction. Use alternative antifungal if possible.

description: Rifampin induces warfarin metabolism, reducing anticoagulant effect.
recommendation: Monitor INR closely. May need significant warfarin dose increase. Consider alternative TB therapy.

description: Amiodarone increases warfarin levels, increasing bleeding risk.
recommendation: Monitor INR closely. Reduce warfarin dose by 30-50%. Monitor for bleeding.

description: NSAIDs reduce cyclosporine clearance and increase nephrotoxicity risk.
recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs. Use acetaminophen. Monitor renal function and cyclosporine levels.

description: NSAIDs increase nephrotoxicity risk with tacrolimus.
recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs. Use acetaminophen. Monitor renal function and tacrolimus levels.

description: Quinolones increase theophylline levels, risk of toxicity.
recommendation: Monitor theophylline levels. May need dose reduction. Use alternative antibiotic if possible.

description: Erythromycin increases theophylline levels, risk of toxicity.
recommendation: Monitor theophylline levels. May need dose reduction. Use alternative antibiotic if possible.

description: Phenobarbital induces warfarin metabolism, reducing anticoagulant effect.
recommendation: Monitor INR closely. May need warfarin dose increase. Consider alternative sedative.

description: Carbamazepine induces warfarin metabolism, reducing anticoagulant effect.
recommendation: Monitor INR closely. May need warfarin dose increase. Consider alternative anticonvulsant.

description: St. John's Wort induces warfarin metabolism, reducing anticoagulant effect.
recommendation: Avoid St. John's Wort. Monitor INR closely if patient insists on use.

description: Ketoconazole inhibits simvastatin metabolism, increasing myopathy risk.
recommendation: Use alternative antifungal or statin. Monitor CK levels if combination necessary.

description: Itraconazole inhibits simvastatin metabolism, increasing myopathy risk.
recommendation: Use alternative antifungal or statin. Monitor CK levels if combination necessary.

description: Protease inhibitors inhibit simvastatin metabolism, increasing myopathy risk.
recommendation: Use pravastatin or rosuvastatin instead. Monitor CK levels.

description: Risk of serotonin syndrome. Potentially life-threatening condition.
recommendation: Avoid combination. Wait 14 days after MAOI before starting venlafaxine, or 7 days after venlafaxine before starting MAOI.

description: Risk of serotonin syndrome.
recommendation: Monitor for serotonin syndrome symptoms. Use lowest effective doses. Consider alternative pain medication.

description: Risk of serotonin syndrome. Potentially life-threatening condition.
recommendation: Avoid combination. Use alternative cough suppressant.

description: Increased CNS depression, respiratory depression, and overdose risk.
recommendation: Avoid alcohol completely while taking benzodiazepines. Educate patient on risks.

description: Increased CNS depression, respiratory depression, and overdose risk.
recommendation: Avoid alcohol completely while taking opioids. Educate patient on risks.
